rationale: The signals show strong, consistent demand around crested gecko morphs—especially cappuccino, lilly white, and axanthic—with rising Google Trends interest (+80–150%) and multiple YouTube guides. However, most of our published work already covers cappuccino genetics, super-cappuccino viability, lilly-white identification, and axanthic strategy. The Google autocomplete data is largely generic (care, diet, enclosure), which duplicates our existing guides. Reddit was silent, and Bluesky traffic is mostly social/lifestyle content with low breeding relevance. I found one genuinely novel angle: the confusion around frappuccino breeding outcomes vs. cappuccino, which shows up in Google Trends, YouTube titles, and forum discussion, but lacks a dedicated post. The "extreme harlequin" trend is also rising (+70%) and has no published slug, but it's a polygenic morph and harder to ground in breeding strategy without live forum debate. I'll propose the frappuccino breeding angle and pass on the rest due to duplication risk or low actionability.
